channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Says He’ll Seek to Replace Immigrant Truck Drivers With Veterans
item.description: The president has pushed to curtail the commercial licenses of immigrants, including legal residents, whom he has blamed for crashes.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:description: President Trump mentioned a plan to crack down on truck drivers in the United States without legal status and to replace them with veterans during a panel at a military investment summit in Carlisle, Pa., on Wednesday.

item.title: Investigators Seek Information From Government Officials as Part of Air Force One Leak Investigation
item.description: After The New York Times reported on security concerns related to the Qatari-donated jet, the F.B.I. sought to speak with several people who flew aboard the plane with President Trump last week and asked for their phones.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Glenn Thrush
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump on July 1 at Joint Base Andrews next to the jet donated by Qatar, before its first flight as Air Force One.

item.title: Suspect in Utah Stabbing Wanted to Kill Muslims, Court Filing Says
item.description: A Muslim man who works at a mall near Salt Lake City is hospitalized with multiple wounds. Charges have not yet been filed against the suspect, who was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto
item.media:description: Syed Sohail Uddin was taken to the hospital and is being closely monitored.

item.title: FBI Evidence Team Visits Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: Officials surveyed the newly drained pool, where the floor began peeling soon after a $16 million repair job. President Trump has blamed vandals.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and David A. Fahrenthold
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Investigators examining the drained Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington on Wednesday.

item.title: Boat That Sank Near Alcatraz Rolled After Wave Struck, Officials Say
item.description: Rescuers described survivors trying to stay afloat in the San Francisco Bay. A brother of the boat’s owner died, and the authorities say they will end the search for the missing.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla, Jacey Fortin and Madaleine Rubin
item.media:description: A San Francisco Fire Department boat searching near Alcatraz Island for three missing people after a recreational boat, the Volare, sank on Tuesday.

item.title: Video Pushed Local Police to Come Clean. D.H.S. Is Ignoring the Lessons.
item.description: The agency has said little about two fatal encounters in the past week. Local police departments have learned to be forthcoming.
item.dc:creator: Shaila Dewan and Esmé E. Deprez
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: A makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo near the site where he was killed in Houston.

item.title: Door-to-Door Search for Grapevines Sold at Costco Begins in California’s Santa Clara County
item.description: Officials in Santa Clara County began knocking on doors on Monday to collect potentially infested plants sold at some Costco stores.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Santa Clara County also warned that desert willows sold at certain Costco locations across 15 Northern California counties between late June 24 and July 3 might also be affected.

item.title: Trump Administration Fires U.S. Attorney Minutes After His Appointment
item.description: Federal judges had chosen the veteran Seattle prosecutor to fill the vacant seat. Now, a potential legal battle looms.
item.dc:creator: Danny Hakim and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.media:description: Roger Rogoff, a former federal prosecutor and state judge, in court in Seattle in 2016.

item.title: G.O.P. Appears to Boost Socialist’s Primary Campaign for Wisconsin Governor
item.description: Republicans seem to view Francesca Hong as their weakest opponent in a general election. They are spending $2.2 million in an apparent attempt to aid her primary campaign.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Some Democrats have fretted that state Representative Francesca Hong, a democratic socialist with unabashedly left-wing views, might win her primary and then deliver the governor’s mansion to Republicans.

item.title: Maine Democrats’ Adventurous Political Experiment
item.description: The party’s leaders have a political experiment on their hands.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood

item.title: Hegseth Plans to Screen All Troops, Including Women, for Low Testosterone
item.description: Pete Hegseth, as defense secretary, has sought to cultivate an image as a manosphere-friendly leader.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Azeen Ghorayshi
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The goal, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a social media message, was a “High-T Department of War.”

item.title: Here Are the Maine Democrats Running to Replace Graham Platner
item.description: To win, they’ll have to convince Democrats at a July 25 convention that they’re the best candidate to take on Senator Susan Collins, a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

item.title: U.S. Military Again Blockades Iranian Ports After Collapse of Cease-Fire
item.description: President Trump ordered U.S. warships and aircraft to stop vessels going to and from Iran. Enforcing a blockade takes a huge commitment of warships and aircraft.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The U.S.S. Abraham Lincoln in San Diego in 2022. According to the U.S. Naval Institute, as of Monday it is anchoring one of two aircraft carrier strike groups deployed in the Northern Arabian Sea.

item.title: Almost Half of House Democrats Vote to End Aid to Israel
item.description: The measure failed, but the level of support among Democrats exposed a stark shift in the party away from backing the Jewish state.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:description: Representative Hakeem Jeffries, the Democratic minority leader and a staunch supporter of Israel, hosted two caucus meetings to discuss the amendment, a highly unusual move that indicated how fraught the politics around Israel have become for Democrats.

item.title: Iran, Epstein, Space Travel: 5 Takeaways From JD Vance’s Interview With Joe Rogan
item.description: In his first on-air conversation with the popular podcaster since 2024, the vice president cautiously defended the Iran war while also hinting at skepticism about it.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance cautiously defended the war with Iran in an interview with Joe Rogan released Wednesday, while also hinting at his own skepticism about the conflict.

item.title: Todd Blanche, Trump’s Attorney General Pick, Faces Crucial Hurdle After Rocky Hearing
item.description: Even a single Republican “no” vote would block Mr. Blanche’s nomination from consideration by the full Senate, which could sink his confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush, Devlin Barrett, Michael Gold and Alan Feuer
item.media:description: Todd Blanche on Wednesday at his confirmation hearing to be attorney general. It is not clear when the Senate Judiciary Committee will schedule a vote.

item.title: Court Filings Describe Ongoing Contact Between ICE and Iran on Deportations
item.description: In a sworn declaration, a lawyer says an Iranian official reported receiving regular dossiers on Iranian detainees from Immigration and Customs Enforcement for months.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
item.media:description: According to one filing, the Iranian official indicated the Trump administration’s goal was to “get rid of all Iranians” held in immigration detention in the United States.

item.title: Inside Maine Democrats’ Search for a New Senate Nominee After Graham Platner’s Exit
item.description: Democratic candidates are hastily building bare-bones campaigns and forgoing traditional outreach like TV ads in a sprint to appeal to party delegates, rather than Maine voters.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Several Maine Democrats are jockeying to become the party’s new Senate nominee after Graham Platner, who won the midterm primary, dropped out last week.

item.title: F.B.I. Searches Houston ICE Shooting Van for Drugs
item.description: Federal investigators believe illegal drugs were inside the vehicle, though the immigration agents involved in the encounter do not appear to have known that.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: Family members embraced next to a makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo at the location where he was shot by an I.C.E. agent in Houston.

item.title: Kevin O’Leary Is Sued by Opponents He Claimed Were Foreign Agents
item.description: Kevin O’Leary, the celebrity investor, accused opponents of his proposed data center near the Great Salt Lake of being agents of China. Now they want compensation.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy
item.media:credit: Kim Raff for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Hansel Valley in Box Elder County. The Bar H Ranch, located in the valley, sold its land and water rights to the developers of the proposed Stratos data center.

item.title: Senators Press for Scrutiny of Blanche’s Role in Spiking Clemency Inquiry
item.description: During his confirmation hearing, the acting attorney general refused to discuss the termination of an investigation into a commutation issued by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, during a Senate confirmation hearing on Wednesday.

item.title: Nevada Governor Drops His Own Name at Traffic Stop and Avoids Ticket
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo was pulled over for a suspected violation but did not receive a citation. That has elicited mixed reactions as he campaigns for re-election.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: via Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department
item.media:description: This image taken from a Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department body camera video shows a police officer pulling over Gov. Joe Lombardo on May 15.

item.title: Treasury to Begin Minting $1 Gold Trump Coin Featuring President’s Face
item.description: Critics question whether the coins are legal and argue that President Trump is using the currency to celebrate himself.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport

item.title: Trump Endorses Mike Lindell, MyPillow Founder, for Minnesota Governor
item.description: He is a close ally of President Trump and has been a leading proponent of the election denial movement, which falsely claims that voting machines are often rigged.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño
item.media:description: Mike Lindell at the Conservative Political Action Conference in Grapevine, Texas, in March.

item.title: Elon Musk Funds Vivek Ramaswamy’s Bid for Ohio Governor Despite Their DOGE Clashes
item.description: Elon Musk gave $5 million to back Vivek Ramaswamy’s campaign for Ohio governor, seemingly moving past tensions around the effort to downsize the federal work force.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times; Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy developed a frosty relationship after President Trump named them as the leaders of the so-called Department of Government Efficiency.

item.title: House G.O.P. Releases Budget to Unlock $95 Billion for Iran War and SAVE Act
item.description: The plan faces a rocky path amid Republican divisions and would pave the way for votes months before the midterm elections to fund a war that polls show is deeply unpopular.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Representative Jodey Arrington, Republican of Texas and the chairman of the House Budget Committee. His panel was set to vote on the plan on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Pressures ICE to Resume Traffic Stops After They Were Halted Over Fatal Shootings
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement had ordered its officers to halt most vehicle stops across the country after they shot two people over the past week.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:description: In a post on social media on Wednesday, President Trump defended the traffic stops and urged ICE to “go back and do your very important job.”

item.title: Blanche’s Family Ties to Gun Industry Are Likely to Raise Questions
item.description: Democrats and gun control advocates are concerned that his comments about Check-Mate Industries, a company led by his mother-in-law that manufactures magazines, reveal a conflict of interest.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting U.S. attorney general.

item.title: What to Know About Todd Blanche’s Order Granting Trump Immunity From I.R.S. Audits
item.description: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche’s decision to grant President Trump protection from I.R.S. audits was a central issue in his confirmation hearing on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump’s extraordinary protection from the I.R.S. is expected to be a central issue in the confirmation hearing on Wednesday.

item.title: A single Republican no vote could sink Blanche’s nomination. Tillis and Cornyn have had doubts.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Alan Feuer
item.media:description: It would take just a single Republican no vote to deadlock the Senate Judiciary Committee, effectively sinking the confirmation of Todd Blanche, who was elevated to acting attorney general when Pam Bondi was fired in April.

item.title: Biden Will Release Memoir Just After the November Midterms
item.description: The former president announced that the book, “Promise Me, America,” will come out on Nov. 17, and will cover the tumultuous events of his presidency as well as his re-election bid.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Little, Brown and Co., via Associated Press
item.media:description: Former President Joseph R. Biden Jr. said his book would cover topics including how he handled the coronavirus pandemic, the economy, conflicts in Afghanistan and Ukraine, and “restoring our democracy.”

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush, Devlin Barrett and Alan Feuer

item.title: Clayton Dodges Question on 2020 Election, and Democrats Call Him on It
item.description: Jay Clayton’s nomination to lead the U.S. intelligence community comes as the White House pushes to use declassified intelligence to show concerns about election security.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.media:description: The Senate Intelligence Committee is expected to vote on Jay Clayton’s nomination early next week.

item.title: Senator Mark Kelly Amasses Nearly $25 Million Campaign War Chest
item.description: The Arizona Democrat said he had raised and given away $10 million to fellow members of his party and committees as he mulls a presidential run in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Mark Kelly’s fund-raising jumped last year after President Trump accused him and five other Democrats of “sedition” over a video in which they told service members they could ignore illegal orders.

item.title: Todd Blanche Faces Crucial Hurdle Ahead of Confirmation as Attorney General
item.description: Todd Blanche’s nomination is broadly a referendum on President Trump’s vision of the Justice Department as a projection of his power.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush, Devlin Barrett and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, during a House hearing last month.

item.title: Small Tribes Say They Lack the Clout to Secure Federal Recognition
item.description: The federal government can confer health care, education, housing and casino rights, but for small bands like many in the Pacific Northwest, recognition takes political muscle they don’t have.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin
item.media:description: “We refuse to say we don’t have federal recognition; the federal government cannot tell me we are or are not Chinook.” Members of the Chinook Indian Nation near Bay Center, Wash.

item.title: Should Epstein’s Friends Be Punished? An Elite Harvard Club Is Torn.
item.description: At Harvard, students, alumni and professors debate whether a storied campus group should cut ties with one of Jeffrey Epstein’s closest associates, and whether Harvard itself should do the same.
item.dc:creator: Debra Kamin
item.media:credit: Jemal Countess/Getty Images for The Hasty Pudding Institute of 1770
item.media:description: Andrew Farkas spoke in 2014 at an event for the Hasty Pudding Institute, a Harvard group. Mr. Farkas has come under criticism for soliciting funds for the group from Jeffrey Epstein.
